title: A Titrimetric Method for the Determination of Oxalic and Malonic Acids in a Mixture
author: S. R. SAGI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Inorganic Chemistry Laboratories, Andhra University, Visakhapatnam-530 003 

  Manuscript received 12 March 1992, revised 26 June 1992, accepted 17 July 1992  

 Oxalic and malonic acids can be oxidised quantitatively to carbon dioxide and water when refluxed for 120 min in >1.5  M  sulphuric acid medium in the presence of four times the thallium(III) required for the quantitative oxidation. In the photochemical oxidation of the mixture only oxalic acid is oxidised while malonic acid remains unaffected when a chloride concentration, three times (or above) that of thallium(III) is present. Basing on this fact a convenient titrimetric method is described for the analysis of mixtures of oxalic and malonic acids. In this method the thallium(I) formed is determined bromatometrically using methyl orange or brilliant ponceaux 5R as indicator. The indicator correction is negligible.
